summary: I-00083's own run did not require any cross-item carry-over fixes; the two fix cycles (S06 lint, S07 assertions) are own-file polish on the newly authored test. The new daemon log line was not emitted on this CR's worktree-create event by design — the daemon is still running pre-fix main code; first emission will occur post-merge after daemon restart. No pre-existing tests were modified under the old chore-commit shape; the new test file is authored under the new (narrow) allow-list. CR-00053's three carry-over fixes (assertion_free_baseline.txt, BatchStatus.executing→completed, _HEAD_REVISION) did not recur in this run.

focus_areas.question: Did this CR's own run need any carry-over fixes?

fix_cycles_observed.cause: PT018 compound assertion in own test file
tautological assertion in own test file

focus_areas.explanation: Both fix cycles were scoped to tests/integration/test_branch_base_drift.py — the newly authored regression suite — and exercised this CR's own quality polish, not cross-item drift. The run was clean because no in-flight sibling in BATCH-00104 was concurrently writing into orch/active_files.py or orch/daemon/batch_manager.py.

focus_areas.question: Was the daemon log line emitted on every worktree-create event, including this CR's?
focus_areas.evidence: grep -E 'worktree create:|sibling_paths_without_merge|in_flight_siblings' logs/daemon.log returned zero matches for I-00083
focus_areas.explanation: The daemon process that created I-00083's worktree at 2026-05-16 05:13 was still running pre-fix main code; the new _emit_sibling_drift_log method only exists in this worktree's working tree. First live emission will occur after the CR merges and the daemon reloads. Same self-bootstrap caveat as CR-00021. S03's TestI00083HappyPathSoloItem + TestI00083SiblingScopeUnit unit tests cover the emission shape in lieu of live evidence.

focus_areas.question: Backwards-compat sanity: were any tests modified that were authored under the old chore-commit shape?
focus_areas.explanation: Only a new file was added. The new file's _simulate_chore_commit helper unconditionally creates all four allow-listed pathspecs because the production allow-list now hard-fails git-add when any pathspec is absent (a real constraint flagged in S03 report L117-124 and S05 H1).

focus_areas.question: Cross-CR pattern vs CR-00053's three concrete carry-over fixes
focus_areas.explanation: None of CR-00053's carry-overs were needed in I-00083's run. The three BatchStatus.executing references in tests/integration/test_branch_base_drift.py (lines 225, 350, 467) are the test's own Batch factory and are unrelated to CR-00053's executing→completed flip in test_dashboard_actions.py. The validating bite remains CR-00053's hand-rescue, referenced in the design doc Notes section.

outstanding_review_findings.recommended_fix: Add 'orch/active_files.py' to scope.allowed_paths in the manifest (S05's preferred resolution).

outstanding_review_findings.summary: git add -- <pathspec> exits 128 when an allow-listed pathspec resolves to nothing; iw approve will regress for any item missing <ID>_Functional.md / workflow-manifest.json / etc.
outstanding_review_findings.recommended_fix: Pre-filter the four pathspecs via Path.glob/is_file/is_dir before passing to git add. The dirty_check already guarantees a non-empty filtered list.
outstanding_review_findings.follow_up: File as a new incident — this is a production-side regression introduced by I-00083.

outstanding_review_findings.summary: _emit_sibling_drift_log body has no outer try/except; a transient DB error in _collect_in_flight_sibling_items can abort _launch_item after the worktree exists on disk but before BatchItemStatus.executing is set, contradicting the v1 WARN-only contract.
outstanding_review_findings.recommended_fix: Wrap the entire _emit_sibling_drift_log body in try/except Exception with logger.warning(..., exc_info=True). One-indent change.

deferred_verifications.what: First live emission of 'worktree create: ...' INFO log line
deferred_verifications.when: Post-merge, after daemon reload, on the next worktree the daemon creates
